data.description:
Repro (verified): build the MCP server with REBAR_MCP_READONLY=1, call fsck with {"recover": true} -> executes ticket-fsck-recover.sh, whose own header says "IMPORTANT — this script IS destructive. It modifies git state" (rebase --continue/--abort, cherry-picks, commits).

Source: mcp_server.py:204-208 (fsck declared in the ungated read section with a recover param); __init__.py:369-371 maps recover=True -> fsck-recover; ticket-fsck-recover.sh:12-16.

Expected: destructive recovery blocked when readonly. Fix: reject recover=True under _readonly(), or split a write-gated fsck_recover tool. RED test: readonly server + fsck(recover=True) -> ToolError; non-readonly still works.
data.title: MCP fsck(recover=True) runs the destructive recovery path under REBAR_MCP_READONLY=1
